replies.msg:
Further reading

美國用法
點解係John 唔係Peter：

It’s not entirely certain, but the most likely answer is that punters were reluctant to give their real name when, say, checking into a hotel with a sex worker. John is a very common name, and often used when giving fake names - it may even be a contraction of “John Smith”, generally regarded to be the ultimate non-descript name.

https://www.quora.com/Why-are-Clients-in-England-called-punters-instead-of-a-John

英國用法
點解係punter：
It's because historically seeing a prostitute has always been something of a gamble*.

Punter can also mean a person who gambles, places a bet, or makes a risky investment.
